decisions: Nest PerGo's metadata under a `pergo_metadata` or `custom_data` object to avoid collisions
Forward raw text exactly as received to preserve user intent
Map PerGo's `SenderIdentity` (or combined) to Typebot's `sessionId` root field
Combine ConnectionID and SenderIdentity (e.g., `<ConnectionID>:<SenderIdentity>`) for the Typebot `sessionId` to ensure uniqueness per channel
Pass the public URL of the media (if available/uploaded by PerGo) for media attachments
Send the media URL as a separate metadata field inside `pergo_metadata.media_url` and use a placeholder for `message`
Use `[Media Attachment]` as the placeholder text in the `message` field
Include the media type (e.g., `image`, `video`, `audio`) explicitly in the `pergo_metadata` payload

For unsupported message types (e.g., location, contact), send a placeholder text (e.g., `[Unsupported message: location]`) in the `message` field to hand off control to Typebot's native fallback/timeout mechanisms
